title: Mariscus ossicaulis
intro: Mariscus ossicaulis, also known as Oshá or Bear's Cloak, is a perennial herb belonging to the Cyperaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Mariscus ossicaulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mariscus ossicaulis is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to address a range of health concerns.
